steps.title: Find the key signal
steps.content: Text 1 argues that "relationships between pieces of music may be best understood with concepts other than genre" and criticizes genre categories as ambiguous and simplistic. The decisive signal is the rejection of genre as a useful framework for musical analysis.

steps.title: Match the meaning
steps.content:
Choice B aligns with the author’s skepticism by asserting genre classifications may not be the most helpful way to think about music.

A and D focus on benefits of technical improvements to classification, which contradict the central claim of Text 1.

C introduces human preference, which the passage does not address.

That matches choice B — B is correct.
